#### [Features\   /](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/content/features) [Editor's Choice](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/content/category/section/editors-choice)

## The Best Classical Albums of 2025

9 December 202520 mins read

[Share](https://www.addtoany.com/share#url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.gramophone.co.uk%2Fcontent%2Ffeatures%2Fthe-best-classical-albums-of-2025-so-far&title=Gramophone%20-%20The%20Best%20Classical%20Albums%20of%202025%20(So%20Far)) [LinkedIn](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/#linkedin) [Teams](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/#microsoft_teams) [WhatsApp](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/#whatsapp) [Facebook](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/#facebook) [Email](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/#email) [X](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/#x)

If you are searching for an inspirational new recording, look no further. All of these outstanding classical albums were Editor's Choices in Gramophone magazine and are highly recommended


![](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/media/kfqlynqr/gramophone-best-of-2025.jpg)

In every issue of _Gramophone_, Editor Martin Cullingford chooses 10 new releases as his Editor's Choice selection. Below, you will find all of the albums selected as Editor's Choice in 2025, with the most recent releases at the top of the list.

Members of the _Gramophone_ Full Club and _Gramophone_ Digital Club can access our Reviews Database, a digital archive of all of our reviews from 1983 to today, with all of the new issue's reviews available on the day of publication. To find out more about subscribing to _Gramophone_ and the Reviews Database, please visit: [**magsubscriptions.com**](https://www.magsubscriptions.com/music-gramophone)

## **![](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/media/p3sk2rnx/lucia-di-lammermoor.jpg)**

## **Donizetti** Lucia di Lammermoor

**Sols; Orchestra of the Teatro Massimo Bellini, Catania / Fabrizio Maria Carminati**(EuroArts)

Soprano Lisette Oropesa excels in one of the pinnacles of the bel canto repertoire, the lead in a superb line-up of singers, in a studio recording produced with panache.

[**Read the _Gramophone_ review**](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/review/donizetti-lucia-di-lammermoor-carminati)

## **![](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/media/1cjdyzcz/ades-elgar.png)**

## **Adès. Elgar** Violin Concertos

**Christian Tetzlaff** _vn_ **BBC Philharmonic Orchestra / John Storgårds**(Ondine)

A revelatory recording of Elgar’s powerful work from Christian Tetzlaff, plus a brilliant interpretation of Thomas Adès’s major modern concerto for the violin.

[**Read the _Gramophone_ review**](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/review/ades-violin-concerto-christian-tetzlaff-leila-josefowicz)

## **![](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/media/ykebrhsp/mahler-symphony-no-2.png)**

## **Mahler** Symphony No 2, ‘Resurrection’

**Sols; Hallé Orchestra / Kahchun Wong**(Hallé)

The latest recording from an orchestra-conductor relationship that’s been capturing the imagination of audiences: Kahchun Wong and the Hallé’s moving Mahler Second.

[**Read the _Gramophone_ review**](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/review/mahler-symphony-no-2-wong)

## **![](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/media/nojdvjz2/ustvolskaya-symphonies.png)**

## **Ustvolskaya** Symphonies

**London Philharmonic Orchestra / Christian Karlsen**(BIS)

These five symphonies offer us a fascinating and superbly performed insight into the music of the 20th-century Russian composer Galina Ustvolskaya.

[**Read the _Gramophone_ review**](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/review/ustvolskaya-symphonies-nos-1-5-karlsen)

## **Brahms** Two String Quartets, Op 51

**Zehetmair Quartet**(ECM New Series)

Familiar repertoire made to feel urgently fresh, from an ensemble – albeit with two changes – that won Recording of the Year in 2002 with an equally compelling album of Schumann.

[**Read the _Gramophone_ review**](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/review/brahms-string-quartets-op-51)

## **Beethoven** Piano Sonatas Nos 28-32

**Paul Wee** _pf_(BIS)

Having hugely impressed in virtuosic but lesser-recorded repertoire, pianist Paul Wee now demonstrates brilliance of colour and technique in Beethoven’s final sonatas, the most core of repertoire.

## **![](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/media/ybchb2c4/mahler-symphony-no-1.jpg)**

## **Mahler** Symphony No 1

**Zurich Tonhalle Orchestra / Paavo Järvi**(Alpha)

A glorious rendition of Mahler’s First Symphony, its exploratory colours and themes brought out as if fresh discoveries by Paavo Järvi and his musicians.

[**Read the _Gramophone_ review**](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/review/mahler-symphony-no-1-jarvi)

## **![](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/media/q5vcn4jp/track-1-novo-quartet.jpg)**

## **Nielsen. Shostakovich. Vestergård**‘Track 1’

**Novo Quartet**(Aparté)

This youthful Danish ensemble offer us versions of Nielsen’s First and Shostakovich’s Eighth quartets which belong alongside the very finest.

[**Read the _Gramophone_ review**](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/review/novo-quartet-track-1-shostakovich-vestergard-nielsen)

## **![](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/media/owslrz1y/bonds-montgomery-variations-still-symphonies-nos-2-4.jpg)**

## **Bonds** Montgomery Variations **Still** Symphonies Nos 2 & 4

**Philadelphia Orchestra / Yannick Nézet-Séguin**(DG)

The melodic skills of William Grant Still and Margaret Bonds are demonstrated with both sensitivity and sumptuousness by Nézet-Séguin and his Philadelphia forces.

[**Read the _Gramophone_ review**](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/review/still-and-bonds-variations-and-symphonies)

## **![](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/media/2l4brsnf/butterworth-a-shropshire-lad-holst-egdon-heath-st-paul-s-suite.jpg)**

## **Butterworth. Holst** Orchestral works

**RLPO / Andrew Manze**(Onyx)

_The Banks of Green Willow_ and _A Shropshire Lad_ from one composer, _Egdon Heath_ and the _St Paul’s Suite_ from the other, plus much more, beautifully played with a moving affection.

[**Read the _Gramophone_ review**](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/review/butterworh-holst-string-music-manze)

**Daniil Trifonov** _pf_(DG)

Works including _Album for the Young_ and the _Sleeping Beauty_ suite, plus much more, all performed with immense style – ‘a high point in Trifonov’s discography’, writes reviewer Jed Distler.

## **Purcell** Dido and Aeneas

_Sols incl_ **DiDonato & Spyres; Il Pomo d’Oro / Maxim Emelyanychev**

(Erato)

A performance of real poignancy and beauty; Joyce DiDonato and Michael Spyres are superb, with Il Pomo d’Oro and Maxim Emelyanychev as highly sensitive partners.

[**Read the _Gramophone_ review**](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/review/purcell-dido-and-aeneas-emelyanychev)

**Guy Johnston** _vc_ **Royal Liverpool Philharmonic Orchestra / Andrew Manze**

## **![](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/media/d0bfzzgl/echoes-of-exile.jpg)**

## **‘Echoes of Exile’**

**Sueye Park** _vn_

(BIS)

Violinist Sueye Park offers a thoughtfully programmed and powerfully performed series of solo works from the 1920s to 1950s, reflecting various composers’ experiences of, and musical responses to, exile.

[**Read the _Gramophone_ review**](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/review/sueye-park-echoes-of-exile)

## ![](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/media/ouabpgyr/monteverdi-il-sesto-libro-de-madrigali-a580-20250822063847-front.jpg)

## **Monteverdi** Il sesto libro de madrigali

**RossoPorpora / Walter Testolin**

(Arcana)

On only their second album, the vocal ensemble RossoPorpora bring a glorious sense of rapport, vision and of course vocal beauty to these Monteverdi madrigals.

[**Read the _Gramophone_ review**](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/review/monteverdi-sixth-book-of-madrigals)

## **![](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/media/rrpadgyy/schubert-quintette-imaginaire.jpg)**

## **Schubert**‘Quintette imaginaire’

**Sandrine Piau** _sop_ **Quatuor Psophos**

(Alpha)

A fascinating idea that works wonderfully – Schubert lieder transcribed for string quartet, the Psophos Quartet adding a unique atmosphere to Sandrine Piau’s expressive brilliance.

[**Read the _Gramophone_ review**](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/review/schubert-quintette-imaginaire-sandrine-piau)

## **![](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/media/xragacxa/monteverdi-il-sesto-libro-de-madrigali-rossoporpora-walter-testolin.jpg)**

## **Saint-Saëns** L’ancêtre

**Sols; Monte-Carlo Philharmonic Orchestra / Kazuki Yamada**

(Bru Zane)

Palazzetto Bru Zane champion another rarely heard Saint-Saëns opera, and the superb artists and exemplary packaging alike are up to this label’s customary high standards.

[**Read the _Gramophone_ review**](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/review/saint-saens-lancetre-yamada)

## **JS Bach**‘King of Kings’ – Orchestral Transcriptions by Andrew Davis

**BBC Philharmonic Orchestra / Martyn Brabbins, Andrew Davis**(Chandos)

On this splendid tribute to Sir Andrew Davis, his Bach transcriptions – initially by him and then completed by Martyn Brabbins – are beautifully realised by the BBC Philharmonic.

[**Read the _Gramophone_ review**](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/review/js-bach-king-of-kings-orch-davis)

## **JS Bach** Six English Suites

**Alexandra Papastefanou** _pf_(First Hand)

These stylish and exploratory performances of Bach’s _English Suites_ from pianist Alexandra Papastefanou feel natural and convincing and reveal her deep connection to this music’s spirit.

## **JS Bach**‘Bach’s Horns’

**Solomon’s Knot**(Prospero)

Three works tied together thematically with great spirit by the excellent baroque and early music collective Solomon’s Knot: an ingenious idea, wonderfully executed and strongly recommended by David Vickers.

[**Read the _Gramophone_ review**](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/review/bachs-horns)

**Julieth Lozano Rolong** _sop_ **João Araújo** _pf_(Somm)

Audience Prize winner at the 2023 Cardiff Singer of the World competition, Julieth Lozano Rolong is a compellingly characterful soprano – and this issue’s One to Watch.

## **![](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/media/hslovz0z/britten-sinfonia-da-requiem-etc-london-philharmonic-orchestra-edward-gardner.jpg)**

## **Britten** Sinfonia da Requiem. Winter Words. The Prince of the Pagodas

**London Philharmonic Orchestra / Edward Gardner**(LPO)

A _Sinfonia da Requiem_ of intense energy, Britten’s early work just one part of a programme also including Robin Holloway’s orchestration of _Winter Words_.

[**Read the _Gramophone_ review**](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/review/britten-prince-of-the-pagodas-winter-words)

## **![](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/media/ru5ms12l/ligeti-concertos-isabelle-faust.jpg)**

## **Ligeti** Concertos

**Isabelle Faust** _vn_ **Jean-Frédéric Neuburger** _pf_ **Les Siècles / François-Xavier Roth** _pf_(Harmonia Mundi)

Isabelle Faust is a formidable soloist in Ligeti’s Violin Concerto; like all her colleagues on this album, she perfectly understands the composer’s sound world.

[**Read the _Gramophone_ review**](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/review/ligeti-violin-concerto-concert-rom-nesc-piano-concerto-roth)

## **![](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/media/1isfavuo/tchaikovsky-orchestral-works-vol-3-bbc-scottish-symphony-orchestra-alpesh-chauhan.jpg)**

## **Tchaikovsky** Orchestral Works, Vol 3

**BBC Scottish Symphony Orchestra / Alpesh Chauhan**(Chandos)

Lesser-performed Tchaikovsky – but after listening to Alpesh Chauhan and the BBC Scottish SO’s superb advocacy, you’re left scratching your head as to why!

[**Read the _Gramophone_ review**](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/review/tchaikovsky-orchestral-works-vol-3-chauhan)

## **Biber**‘Just Biber’

**Rachel Podger** _vn_ **Brecon Baroque**(Channel Classics)

Rachel Podger’s _Rosary Sonatas_ recording was a _Gramophone_ Award winner; she continues her celebration of Biber with a choice of sonatas that reveal once more her immense affinity with his music.

[**Read the _Gramophone_ review**](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/review/biber-just-biber-rachel-podger)

**A Nocte Temporis / Reinoud Van Mechelen**(Château de Versailles)

## **![](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/media/tagk4f42/sibelius-symphony-no-5-etc-christian-tetzlaff.jpg)**

## **Sibelius** Symphony No 5, etc

**Christian Tetzlaff** _vn_ **Finnish Radio Symphony Orchestra / Nicholas Collon**(Ondine)

A wonderful Fifth, alive to the beauty of Sibelius’s art, perfectly paired with his violin music played by Christian Tetzlaff.

[**Read the review**](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/review/sibelius-symphony-no-5-two-serenades-collon)

## **![](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/media/0p5n2ye0/tchaikovsky-symphony-no-6-etc-royal-liverpool-philharmonic-orchestra-domingo-hindoyan.jpg)**

## **Tchaikovsky** Symphony No 6, etc

**Royal Liverpool Philharmonic Orchestra / Domingo Hindoyan**(Onyx)

The RLPO have found a perfect match in conductor Domingo Hindoyan, this Tchaikovsky _Pathétique_ revealing an orchestra in superb form.

[**Read the review**](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/review/tchaikovsky-souvenir-de-florence-symphony-no-6-hindoyan)

**Pavel Kolesnikov, Samson Tsoy** _pf_(Harmonia Mundi)

**Sols; Munich Radio Orchestra / Ivan Repušić**(BR Klassik)

**Yevgeny Sudbin** _pf_(BIS)

**Lise Davidsen,** **Gerald Finley; Chorus & Orchestra of Norwegian Opera / Edward Gardner**

## ![](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/media/gmehquvs/colouring-book-george-xiaoyuan-fu.jpg)

## ‘Colouring Book’

**George Xiaoyuan Fu** _pf_

Label: Platoon

George Xiaoyuan Fu’s Debussy Études are full of, appropriately, colour, the pianist exploring them with individuality. The modern works – including his own _Passacaglia on a Theme by Radiohead_ – perfectly complete the album.

[**Read the _Gramophone_ review**](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/review/george-xiaoyuan-fu-colouring-book)

## ![](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/media/t4sfjmpe/liszt-via-crucis-etc-leif-ove-andsnes.jpg)

## LISZT Via Crucis & Solo Piano Works

**Leif Ove Andsnes** _pf_ **Norwegian Soloists’ Choir / Grete Pedersen**

Label: Sony Classical

An intensely reflective atmosphere surrounds this performance of Liszt’s astonishing meditation on the Stations of the Cross from Leif Ove Andsnes and colleagues.

[**Read the _Gramophone_ review**](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/review/liszt-via-crucis-solo-piano-works-leif-ove-andsnes)

## ![](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/media/g4xdzcgx/verdi-simon-boccanegra-1857-version-sols-halle-orchestra-sir-mark-elder.jpg)

## VERDI Simon Boccanegra (1857 version)

**Sols; Hallé Orchestra / Sir Mark Elder**

Label: Opera Rara

We delved into the background of Verdi’s original 1857 score for _Simon Boccanegra_ last month, and the resulting recording from Opera Rara is every bit as compelling as hoped.

[**Read the _Gramophone_ review**](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/review/verdi-simon-boccanegra-original-1857-version-elder)

## **![](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/media/vrkmlswj/puccini-tosca-sols-orchestra-of-the-accademia-nazionale-di-santa-cecilia-daniel-harding-dg.jpg)**

## **Puccini** Tosca

**Sols; Orchestra of the Accademia Nazionale di Santa Cecilia / Daniel Harding**(DG)

A triumphant Tosca from DG featuring a superb Eleonora Buratto in the lead and tenor Jonathan Tetelman as Cavaradossi; Daniel Harding conducts the score with real drama.

[**Read the _Gramophone_ review**](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/review/puccini-tosca-harding)

## **![](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/media/xvvjzfem/liszt-rachmaninov-dies-irae-svetlanov-symphony-orchestra-dmitry-masleev.jpg)**

## **Liszt. Rachmaninov**‘Dies irae’

**Svetlanov Symphony Orchestra / Dmitry Masleev** _pf_(Aparté)

Events saw Dmitry Masleev conduct from the piano, and the result is full of rapport, and some spectacular music-making.

[**Read the _Gramophone_ review**](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/review/dies-irae-liszt-rachmaninov-dmitry-masleev)

## **![](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/media/h00b2luj/walton-violin-concerto-etc-charlie-lovell-jones-vn-sinfonia-of-london-john-wilson.jpg)**

## **Walton** Violin Concerto, etc

**Charlie Lovell-Jones** _vn_ **Sinfonia of London / John Wilson**(Chandos)

A brilliant start to a series of Walton orchestral works under John Wilson – each will offer a concerto, and Charlie Lovell-Jones delivers that for violin in great style.

[**Read the _Gramophone_ review**](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/review/walton-violin-concerto-portsmouth-point-suite-from-troilus-and-cressida-wilson)

## **Machaut**‘A Lover’s Death’

**The Orlando Consort**(Hyperion)

After 11 installments the Orlando Consort’s recording of Machaut’s complete songs ends as impeccably as it began; the composer could not have asked for better advocacy from a modern ensemble.

## **![](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/media/g22ditk5/hough-piano-concerto-sir-stephen-hough.png)**

## **Hough** Piano Concerto

**Sir Stephen Hough** _pf_ **Hallé Orchestra / Sir Mark Elder**(Hyperion)

A major new addition from Sir Stephen Hough to his growing list of works – a piano concerto, which he performs with all his customary colour, flair and elegance.

## **![](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/media/1ytazwno/gerontius-collon.jpg)**

## **Elgar** The Dream of Gerontius

**Sols; Finnish Radio Symphony Orchestra / Nicholas Collon**(Ondine)

Nicholas Collon conducts a captivating interpretation of Elgar’s deeply spiritual work, always alert to its epic drama, while John Findon is a compelling Gerontius.

[**Read the review**](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/review/elgar-the-dream-of-gerontius-collon)

**Ensemble Peregrina; Ensemble Dragma / Agnieszka Budzińska‑Bennett**(Raumklang)

## **Mahler** Symphony No 7

**Bavarian Radio Symphony Orchestra / Sir Simon Rattle**(BR Klassik)

## **Smetana** Orchestral Works

**Prague Radio Symphony Orchestra / Petr Popelka**(Supraphon)

From the well-known _Má vlast_ to lesser-known works, this is a rewarding in-depth exploration of Smetana’s orchestral music from Petr Popelka and his Prague players.

[**Read the _Gramophone_ review**](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/review/smetana-ma-vlast-symphonic-works-popelka)

## **Bartók. Ligeti** String Quartets

**Marmen Quartet**(BIS)

A superb album from the Award-winning Marmen Quartet, their cohesion of vision and vibrant virtuosity on stunning display in this collection of Hungarian works from Bartók and Ligeti.

[**Read the _Gramophone_ review**](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/review/bartok-string-quartet-no-4-ligeti-string-quartets-nos-2-and-4)

## **Beethoven** String Quartets, Op 18

**Calidore Quartet**(Signum)

The Calidore Quartet have drawn plaudits throughout this Beethoven cycle – the middle quartets a Recording of the Month – and this early set is a wonderful journey’s end (or, rather, beginning).

## **‘Italophilia’**

**The Counterpoints**(Challenge Classics)

Music-making of absolute grace and individuality runs throughout this exquisite album from Early Music collective The Counterpoints, which explores the influence of Italy on London musical life of the 17th and 18th centuries.

## **![](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/media/n3ijuvbd/seelentrost-isabel-schicketanz.png)**

## **‘Seelentrost’**

**Isabel Schicketanz** _sop_(Perfect Noise)

A wonderful debut solo recital album from soprano Isabel Schicketanz, which explores early Baroque music about the soul, whether at times of distress, loneliness or loss, and all delivered with captivating intimacy.

[**Read the _Gramophone_ review**](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/review/seelentrost-the-sound-of-inner-life-in-heinrich-schutz-time-isabel-schicketanz)

## **JS Bach** Eleven Organ Chorales

**Masaaki Suzuki** _org_(BIS)

A glorious organ recital from Masaaki Suzuki, continuing his Bach survey, recorded on an instrument (in the Martinikerk, Groningen) perfectly chosen to reflect the dynamic breadth of the pieces.

[**Read the _Gramophone_ review**](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/review/js-bach-organ-works-vol-6-masaaki-suzuki)

## **‘Lucrezia’**

**Sols; Les Paladins / Jérôme Correas**(Aparté)

Four Baroque cantatas from different composers that tackle the story of Lucrezia, brought dramatically to life by some superb singers, plus Les Paladins on fine form under Jérôme Correas.

[**Read the _Gramophone_ review**](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/review/lucrezia-portraits-of-a-woman)

**The Brabant Ensemble / Stephen Rice**(Hyperion)

Stephen Rice’s Brabant Ensemble present an engaging programme of music exploring monastic life, including lesser-known composers from the post-Reformation German-speaking world.

## **![](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/media/n22bypae/17-voices-of-thunder.jpg)**

## **‘Voices of Thunder’**

**Choir of Magdalen College, Oxford / Mark Williams**(Coro)

The new Hermann Eule organ of Magdalen College, Oxford is presented in all its glory in music leading up the present day, the choir shining just as gloriously.

[**Read the _Gramophone_ review**](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/review/voices-of-thunder)

[Editor's Choice](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/content/category/section/editors-choice)
